food_subgroup: Combined Italian meal set — baked pasta with cheese-meat sauce paired with sourdough-base pizza

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a combined lasagne-and-pizza meal set. FOODON_00002572 (pizza food product) is used as the closest parent for the pizza component. The lasagne component maps to baked pasta dish, but no combined-meal-set term exists in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Pizza) is used as the closest parent anchor for the pizza component of this combo meal. No FoodEx2 code exists for a combined lasagne-pizza meal set. Lasagne would map to pasta dishes (A00KP) but the combined set has no single FoodEx2 code.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226789007 (Pizza) used as closest parent for the pizza component. No SNOMED CT concept exists for a combined lasagne-and-pizza meal bundle.

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is approximately 600 g, comprising a standard lasagne portion (approximately 300 g) and a personal or sharing sourdough pizza (approximately 300 g), as served at Now Pizza – Funan, Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Lasagna with meat sauce, baked — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; Pizza, cheese, regular crust — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; combined weighted estimate
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct approved-source entry exists for a combined lasagne-and-pizza combo meal. Nutrient values are estimated as a 50:50 weighted blend of (1) USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for 'Lasagna with meat sauce, baked' (FDC ID 172004, approximately 166 kcal/100 g) and (2) USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for 'Pizza, cheese, regular crust' (FDC ID 170000, approximately 266 kcal/100 g). The blended per-100 g values represent the composite meal. Sourdough base is treated as equivalent to regular pizza crust for nutrient estimation purposes. Confidence is medium due to estimation from components.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Pasta dishes and pizza entries; Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a combined lasagne-and-pizza combo meal. GI is estimated as a weighted average of (1) lasagne GI ≈ 47 (University of Sydney GI Database; lasagna, meat sauce, baked, tested value) and (2) pizza with regular crust GI ≈ 49 (University of Sydney GI Database; pizza, cheese, thin crust). Weighted 50:50 blend yields GI ≈ 48, classified as low (≤55). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (48 × 148.8) / 100 ≈ 71.4, where carbohydrate per 600 g serving = 24.8 × 6 = 148.8 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg/100 g; ~2880 mg per 600 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: A single 600 g combo serving provides approximately 2880 mg sodium, exceeding the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size, request reduced-salt preparation, or avoid this meal on days when other sodium-containing foods are consumed.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load per serving (~71) despite low GI (~48), driven by large carbohydrate quantity in a full combo portion
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although the GI of this combo is low (~48), the large carbohydrate load per full serving (~149 g) produces a high glycemic load (~71); individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider sharing the combo, reducing the pizza portion, or pairing with a non-starchy vegetable side to blunt postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~3.8 g/100 g; ~22.8 g per serving) and high sodium from cheese, meat ragù, and processed pizza components
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of saturated fat from mozzarella and meat ragù (~22.8 g per 600 g serving) and high sodium (~2880 mg per serving) presents a moderate cardiovascular risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this meal to occasional consumption and choose smaller portions or lower-fat topping variants where available.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Lasagne and Pizza Combo is a Western Italian-style meal bundle served at Now Pizza – Funan in Singapore, combining two classic Italian baked dishes into a single sharing set. The lasagne component consists of layered flat pasta sheets alternating with a rich meat ragù (typically beef or a beef-pork blend) and creamy béchamel sauce, topped with melted mozzarella cheese and oven-baked until golden and bubbling. The pizza component is built on a sourdough-leavened base, giving it a slightly tangy, chewy crust with a crisp underside, topped with tomato sauce, mozzarella, and a selection of toppings. Together the combo delivers a hearty, calorie-dense meal with a moderate glycemic index (~48, low GI) but a high glycemic load per full serving (~71) due to the large carbohydrate quantity from both pasta and pizza dough. The meal is high in sodium (~480 mg/100 g) primarily from cheese, cured meat, and tomato-based sauces, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. It provides meaningful protein (~10 g/100 g) from meat and dairy, and moderate saturated fat (~3.8 g/100 g). This combo is best suited for sharing between two people to moderate portion size and nutrient load. Clinically, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be aware of the total carbohydrate load per serving, and those with hypertension or cardiovascular disease should monitor sodium and saturated fat intake.

confidence_reason: No direct approved-source entry exists for this specific restaurant combo meal. Nutrient values are estimated from a 50:50 weighted blend of USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for lasagne with meat sauce and cheese pizza with regular crust. GI is estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries for lasagne and cheese pizza. Confidence is medium because the estimation methodology is defensible and sourced from approved databases, but the exact recipe, portion weights, and topping composition at Now Pizza – Funan are not independently verified.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are a 50:50 weighted blend of USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: 'Lasagna with meat sauce, baked' (FDC ID 172004) and 'Pizza, cheese, regular crust' (FDC ID 170000). Actual values may differ based on restaurant recipe, topping selection, and portion split. 2. Serving size of 600 g is an estimate: 300 g lasagne portion + 300 g pizza; actual restaurant portion may vary. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entries used for this estimation. 4. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entries used for this estimation. 5. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entries used for this estimation. 6. GI of 48 is an analogous estimate: weighted average of lasagne GI ≈ 47 and cheese pizza GI ≈ 49 from the University of Sydney GI Database. 7. Glycemic load per serving ≈ 71.4 is calculated as (48 × 148.8) / 100, where 148.8 g is the estimated carbohydrate content of a 600 g serving. 8. Halal status is not confirmed for Now Pizza – Funan; the restaurant is not listed as a certified halal establishment. 9. Sourdough pizza base is treated as nutritionally equivalent to regular pizza crust for estimation purposes. 10. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean market context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as this is a Singapore-served dish.
